blockers.title: Spec-required no-DOB migration path was removed
blockers.evidence: SEC-1 explicitly says `createPendingMember` must keep an optional DOB for the internal migration path so the 1,309 imported members without DOB land in `restricted_unknown`. The implementation changed `createPendingMember` to require `dobAnswer: v.string()` in `convex/members.ts:260-267`, and repo search shows no alternate mutation that creates no-DOB migrated member rows. `deriveAgeBlock(undefined)` still exists, but no changed creation path can call it for migration.
blockers.required_fix: Keep public `submitJoin` strict, but add or restore a migration-only internal member creation path that accepts missing DOB, marks the member as migrated or claim-pending as appropriate, derives `restricted_unknown`, and cannot be reached from public join. Add a regression test for a no-DOB migrated member.

blockers.title: CSP allows all Convex tenants, not the deployment origin
blockers.evidence: SEC-6 requires CSP for self plus the Convex deployment origin. `public/_headers:16` uses `https://*.convex.cloud`, `wss://*.convex.cloud`, and `https://*.convex.site`, which permits arbitrary Convex deployments rather than the WAI-ME deployment. That weakens CSP as a data-exfiltration control.
blockers.required_fix: Restrict CSP to the actual production Convex cloud and site origins, or generate `_headers` per deployment from the configured `PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL` and matching auth/storage origin. Keep Turnstile, Google Fonts, and Unsplash allowances as specified.

non_blocking_notes: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. `npm test` and `npm run check` could not complete in this read-only sandbox because Vitest tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/...` and Astro tried to write `.astro/content.d.ts`.
Added member-facing strings in this diff are plain language and contain no em dashes. Pre-existing em dashes remain in touched portal files for a later brand/copy slice.

The diff includes a stale `.codex/audits/security-hardening.review.json` containing old DO-NOT-SHIP findings that this branch partially fixed. It is not runtime-impacting, but it should not be treated as the current Gate 4 result.

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-1 DOB required at public join boundary
spec_compliance.evidence: `submitJoin` requires and validates DOB, but `createPendingMember` no longer keeps the optional DOB migration path required by the spec.
`beforeSessionCreation` maps `minor` to `pending_guardian`, `restricted_unknown` to `pending_review`, and only calls `issueMembershipCertificate` when `next === "active"`.

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-2 rate limiting on magic-link sends
spec_compliance.evidence: `afterUserCreatedOrUpdated` enforces the 15-minute, 24-hour, and global buckets for `type === "email"` before the verification code write completes; the client maps `rate_limited` to plain wait-and-retry copy.
`updateProfile` reads `_storage` metadata, allows only jpeg/png/webp, rejects files over 5 MB, and returns before patching on invalid photos.
`writeConsent` refuses `pipeline=true` for `minor` and `restricted_unknown`, writes an audit row, returns an error envelope, and does not insert a consent row.
Header families exist, but the CSP uses wildcard Convex origins instead of the deployment origin required by the spec.
`@convex-dev/auth` is pinned exactly to `0.0.94` in `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, with a comment in `convex/auth.ts` explaining why.

spec_compliance.criterion: QA-1 unit tests exist in the repo
spec_compliance.evidence: Vitest suites are committed for age, lane evaluation, lifecycle, profile validation/completeness, rate-limit window logic, and Convex integration regressions. Execution was blocked by read-only sandbox writes.

spec_compliance.criterion: QA-2 CI runs the type gate and unit tests
spec_compliance.evidence: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `npm run check` and `npm test` before installing Playwright and running E2E.
No new runtime pages or design rewrites were added; UI changes are limited to hardening states and error handling.

summary: Do not ship. The main hardening behavior is mostly present, but two acceptance criteria are not actually met: the no-DOB migration path required by SEC-1 is gone, and the CSP is broader than the specified Convex deployment origin.
